name: Green Tārā Practice (Swift Liberator Sādhana)
name_sanskrit: Śyāmatārā — Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Svāhā
primary_purpose: Swift liberation from the eight great fears (internal and external); removal of obstacles; quickening of spiritual progress

mantras.use: Primary Green Tārā mantra — Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Svāhā

visualizations: Green Tārā appears in the space before you, seated on a white moon disc atop a pink lotus throne
Her body is radiant emerald green (#00A86B), the color of vibrant, swift compassionate activity
Her right hand rests on her right knee in the mudrā of supreme giving (varada), palm open, fingers pointing down — bestowing boons and fearlessness
Her left hand is held at her heart in the mudrā of the Three Jewels (thumb touching ring finger), holding the stem of a blue utpala lotus that blossoms at her shoulder
Her face is serene, youthful, and mildly smiling; her eyes wide with compassionate alertness
Her right leg is extended forward (ready to spring into action to help beings); her left leg is drawn up in meditation posture
She is adorned with silken robes, jeweled ornaments, and her hair is bound in a topknot with some falling over her shoulders
From her heart, emerald-green light radiates outward in all directions, instantly reaching every being in distress — dissolving fear, danger, illness, and obstacles into pure radiant light
Visualize the green light returning to your heart, filling you with Tārā's swift, fearless, compassionate energy
benefits: Swift protection from the eight great fears (lions, elephants, fire, snakes, thieves, water, captivity, demons — each also a metaphor for pride, ignorance, anger, hatred, poverty, attachment, doubt, and delusion)
Rapid removal of outer obstacles (dangers, difficulties) and inner obstacles (fear, anxiety, doubt)
Quickening of spiritual progress — Tārā acts with the speed of thought
Awakening of active compassion that moves to relieve suffering
Healing of illness, especially anxiety-related disorders
Liberation from fearful and stuck states of mind
Connection to the Mother of All Buddhas — the feminine wisdom aspect
Accumulation of vast merit through swift compassionate activity
frequency_purpose: Solfeggio frequency of love and DNA repair — resonates with Tārā's heart-opening, swift compassionate energy
twenty_one_taras_note: Green Tārā is the source of the 21 Tārās praised in the 'Praises to the Twenty-One Tārās'. Reciting her mantra connects to all 21 emanations.
best_times: Daily — traditionally recited 108 times each morning for swift protection through the day
On Tārā's feast days (25th lunar day — Dakini Day; full moon)
When in danger, fear, or facing obstacles
When swift resolution of a difficulty is needed
At the request of others who are suffering
On Wednesdays (traditionally associated with Tārā in some lineages)
dedication: By the merit of reciting Tārā's mantra, may I and all beings be swiftly liberated from all fears, outer and inner. May obstacles dissolve, may compassionate action arise, and may all beings attain the swift awakening of Green Tārā. Oṃ Tāre Tuttāre Ture Svāhā.
